changelog:
True persistent statistics (SQLite, kept forever).

All request statistics now persist to a SQLite file (data/stats.db, WAL mode) so they survive container restarts. Three concurrent rollups per request — global, per-model, per-key — keyed by date so per-day breakdowns are queryable indefinitely.

What's persisted:
- Global counters (Total Requests / Success / Failed / Tokens / Credits) — top dashboard cards
- Per-model totals — Analytics tab "Usage by model"
- Per-key lifetime totals already persist in config.json (since A7); now mirrored in stats.db too for symmetric queries
- Per-day rows for time-series charts (one row per (date, scope_type, scope_id))

New endpoints:
  GET /admin/api/stats/totals                         global lifetime totals
  GET /admin/api/stats/history?scope=global&days=28   per-day series
  GET /admin/api/stats/history?scope=model&id=...     per-model series
  GET /admin/api/stats/history?scope=key&id=...       per-key series
  GET /admin/api/modelstats                           updated to read from SQLite

On startup, the handler seeds its in-memory counters from SQLite so the dashboard's top stat cards show the true historical totals immediately, not zeros from a fresh process.

Docker: Go builder bumped to golang:1.25-alpine (modernc.org/sqlite v1.34.5 dependency tree). Image footprint grows ~3 MB for the pure-Go SQLite driver. No CGO required.

A13: highly customizable api keys.
A12: unify api keys (legacy auto-migrates).
A11: WebSocket transport for /v1/responses.
A10: dashboard API Keys + Analytics tabs.
A9: entrypoint chown + su-exec hotfix.
A8: dashboard credits use account-true quotaUsed.
A7: backend multi API keys.
A6: security + reliability hardening.
